title: NordVPN Review 2026: The Best Value VPN, but Not Without Flaws
description: NordVPN offers 6400+ servers, RAM-only infrastructure, and unique features like Meshnet. Here is an honest look at what it does well and where it falls short in 2026.
content:
NordVPN Review 2026: The Best Value VPN, but Not Without Flaws

NordVPN is the most widely recommended VPN for general users, and the 2026 version gives you a lot for the price. At around $3.09 per month on a 2-year plan, it undercuts most premium competitors while matching or beating them on core features. That said, it is not perfect, and a few rough edges are worth knowing before you commit.

Overview: What You Get

NordVPN runs more than 6,400 servers across 111 countries. That is one of the largest server footprints of any consumer VPN. Coverage is particularly strong in Europe and North America, which is where most users need reliable exits. The 2-year plan costs roughly $3.09 per month, which is hard to beat for a service at this tier. A 30-day money-back guarantee is included on all plans.

The app is available on Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Linux, and browser extensions. You can connect up to 10 devices simultaneously on a single subscription.

Speed: Fast Nearby, Variable at Distance

NordVPN uses NordLynx, its own protocol built on top of WireGuard. On nearby servers, this delivers excellent speeds. In testing from Western Europe to European exit nodes, speeds consistently held above 85 percent of the base connection. That is enough for 4K streaming, gaming, and large file transfers without noticeable slowdown.

On long-distance connections, say Europe to Southeast Asia or Australia, the picture changes. Speeds can drop to 40-60 percent of base, which is normal for any VPN at that distance but worth noting if you regularly need cross-continental connections. For most everyday use cases, NordVPN is fast enough that you will rarely notice it is on.

Threat Protection Pro: More Than Just a VPN

Threat Protection Pro is one of NordVPN's standout additions. It blocks malware downloads, trackers, and intrusive ads at the network level, not just in the browser. On some plans, it works independently of the VPN tunnel, meaning you get the protection even when you are not actively connected to a VPN server.

This matters because it turns NordVPN from a simple IP-changer into a broader privacy tool. The malware blocking is particularly useful on mobile, where dedicated ad-blocking is harder to set up. Threat Protection Pro does not require a separate subscription on plans that include it.

Meshnet: A Genuinely Unique Feature

Meshnet lets you create a private encrypted network between your own devices, or between your devices and trusted friends or colleagues. Each device gets a unique NordVPN IP address and can route traffic through any other device in the mesh.

In practice, this is useful for accessing your home network remotely, setting up a private gaming network without port-forwarding headaches, or securely connecting with a small team. No competing VPN at this price point offers anything comparable. Meshnet is free even without an active VPN subscription, which makes it a low-risk feature to try.

RAM-Only Servers: No Logs by Design

NordVPN has migrated its entire server fleet to RAM-only hardware. Servers that run entirely in memory cannot physically retain data after a reboot. Even if a server were seized by authorities, there is nothing to extract.

This is not just a marketing claim. It is a structural guarantee. NordVPN's no-logs policy has been independently audited by Deloitte and PricewaterhouseCoopers. The 2018 server breach, which was a genuine black mark on the company's transparency record, led directly to this infrastructure overhaul. The way the company responded to that incident and what it built afterward is a reasonable basis for trust now.

What NordVPN Does Not Do Well

App Load Times

The desktop app, particularly on Windows, can be slow to launch and connect. On older hardware, the time from opening the app to being connected can stretch to 10-15 seconds. Once connected, performance is fine. But if you frequently toggle the VPN on and off, the startup lag gets irritating. This has been a recurring complaint across multiple app versions and does not appear to be a priority fix for NordVPN.

Customer Support Response Times

NordVPN offers 24/7 live chat support, which is a genuine advantage over some competitors. However, wait times in the chat queue can reach 15-20 minutes during peak hours. For straightforward questions, the help center is good. For anything that requires a human, be prepared to wait. Email support is slower still, typically 24-48 hours for a first response.

Who Is NordVPN Best For?

  • Privacy-focused users who want RAM-only infrastructure and audited no-logs, but do not need the extreme privacy of Mullvad or ProtonVPN.
  • Value seekers who want a premium VPN without paying premium prices. At $3.09/mo on a 2-year plan, NordVPN is hard to argue with.
  • Power users who want features like Meshnet, Threat Protection Pro, obfuscated servers, and split tunneling all in one service.
  • Streamers who need reliable Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and Hulu unblocking across multiple countries.

How It Compares

If you are deciding between NordVPN and its closest rivals, read our ExpressVPN vs NordVPN 2026 comparison for a side-by-side on speed, price, and privacy. For a budget-focused comparison, our Surfshark review 2026 covers how it stacks up at a similar price point.

Verdict

NordVPN in 2026 is the right choice for most people. The server count is large, the speeds are strong for everyday use, the privacy infrastructure is credible, and features like Meshnet and Threat Protection Pro add real value. The app's slow load times and stretched support queues are real annoyances, but they do not undermine the core product. At $3.09 per month, the value is difficult to match.

If absolute privacy is your top priority, look at Mullvad. If budget is your only constraint, Surfshark comes close. For everyone else, NordVPN earns its spot at the top of the list.
